what_it_does: This JSON file is an interpretive ledger entry for app/ai/ai-glyphic.js in the claude-ds project. It captures a neutral, file-local reading of an IIFE that registers two CV_AI capabilities (glyphic.generate and glyphic.save), documenting prompt construction, candidate parsing, schema validation, the save path into CV_ICONS, and associated themes, conventions, concerns, and embedding summaries.
observations: Documents two registered capabilities: glyphic.generate (candidate symbol generator) and glyphic.save (commit chosen candidate to CV_ICONS).
Records that glyphic.generate builds a multi-step prompt threading prior turns (p.thread) and the live CV_ICONS.taxonomy domains/kinds.
States the prompt enforces a strict SVG body style: 24x24, fill none, stroke currentColor, width 1.5, round caps, no <svg> wrapper.
Notes parseCandidates extracts the first JSON array via regex, JSON.parses it, and validates each record with window.CV_GLYPHIC.validateSymbol when available.
Records glyphic.save calls CV_ICONS.add with provenance 'vi' and returns { saved: id }.
Captures that glyphic.generate prefers AI.complete over a.provider.complete and throws if no completion provider exists.
Notes both capabilities use surfaces ['icons','glyphics','*'] with { silent: true } and that glyphic.save has provider null and behaviours [].
Includes a header claim that record shape, write path, and voice are single-sourced elsewhere; this file owns only prompt and parse.
Records a stylistic divergence: the interpreted code uses 'use strict' and var rather than const/arrow.
Notes the interpreted code logs console.error and returns when CV_AI is absent instead of throwing.
Provides embedding-ready summary and intention fields plus fields for purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, and contribution.
standouts: The generation prompt is built from the live taxonomy (domains/kinds), constraining candidates to the existing system vocabulary.
The generate capability is described as a multi-step conversational loop that threads prior turns.
A strict inline SVG-body design language is baked directly into the prompt.
Validation is delegated to CV_GLYPHIC.validateSymbol rather than re-declared in this capability.
conventions: Follows the interpretive ledger schema with project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, and embedding fields.
Maintains a neutral, file-local tone without ranking or declaring canonical winners.
Uses concerns to flag divergences from stated project discipline (e.g., inert load guard vs loud-fail).
Records embedding-ready dense summaries separate from richer interpretive prose.
concerns: Makes cross-file comparisons (e.g., to ai-capabilities-canvas.js) without including those files' content, so the comparison cannot be verified locally.
The symbols array is empty even though the format expects code symbols to be listed.
Some fields (intention_signals, novelty, contribution) blend inference with description, which may affect neutrality.
Embedding summaries compress nuanced behavior and could lose subtle distinctions.
notes: A neutral interpretive ledger entry that documents the glyphic AI capabilities, their prompt/parse logic, and the single-sourcing/vocabulary constraints around icon generation.
questions: What is the exact schema enforced by CV_GLYPHIC.validateSymbol?
How is the multi-step threading formatted inside the prompt (user/assistant turn structure)?
What does the provenance value 'vi' signify?
Why is the symbols array left empty in this interpretive entry?
How is the completion provider selected when AI.complete is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file presents itself as an interpretive layer for ai-glyphic.js and contains the expected interpretive fields; it documents the code's stated purpose and behavior consistently.
apparent_themes: Interpretive documentation as a first-class ledger artifact
Registry-way wiring of AI features as capabilities
Single-sourcing of schema, write path, and voice
Strict design-language constraints embedded in prompts
intention_signals: To create a searchable, neutral summary of the glyphic AI capabilities so downstream fusion and query can understand what the code contributes, how it constrains generation, and where it diverges from project conventions.
novelty: An interpretive layer that captures not only code behavior but inferred intent, themes, and embedding-ready summaries for a registered AI capability.
contribution: Contributes a structured, neutral interpretation of the glyphic generate/save capabilities, including their prompt construction, validation delegation, save path, stylistic divergences, and embedding summaries, for later fusion and query.
summary_for_embedding: Neutral interpretive ledger JSON for app/ai/ai-glyphic.js documenting two CV_AI capabilities (glyphic.generate and glyphic.save), their taxonomy-aware multi-step prompt, strict SVG-body spec, schema-validated save to CV_ICONS, and single-sourcing conventions.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a concise, queryable interpretation of the glyphic icon-generation capabilities and their design-language/taxonomy constraints for system fusion.
